layer3_inference: SCADA may be running internal diagnostics on the tripped pump to determine restart readiness
rejection_reason: Layer 3 correctly classified this as SPECULATIVE with 3 assumptions and low confidence, and correctly noted it has been stripped 6+ times across the corrections chain. Layer 4 confirms the rejection. No data supports this inference. Per CL-001, CL-004, CL-010, CL-011, CL-015, CL-018, this inference class has been exhaustively vetted and consistently rejected. The pump is TRIPPED_OFFLINE and that is the entirety of the observable state.

layer3_inference: Operators may be performing off-log activities including FD coordination, isolation valve preparation, or regulatory notification
rejection_reason: Layer 3 correctly classified this as SPECULATIVE with 3 assumptions and low confidence, and correctly noted it is the most thoroughly vetted and consistently rejected inference in the analysis chain (10+ corrections ledger entries). Layer 4 confirms the rejection. The operator log contains three entries. That is the extent of observable operator behavior. No inference about off-log activity is permitted.

layer3_inference: Demand source hidden actions — any inference about unidentified entity behavior
rejection_reason: Layer 3 correctly classified this as SPECULATIVE with 4 assumptions on an unidentified entity. Layer 4 confirms the rejection. You cannot infer the strategy of an actor you cannot identify. Per CL-017, this is structurally unscoreable.
